flavorText: Moon druids master powerful shapeshifting forms for frontline combat.

features.description: You can use Wild Shape as a bonus action, cast certain spells while shifted, and expend spell slots to heal 1d8 hit points per slot level while in Wild Shape.
Your Wild Shape CR limit increases to CR 3 at 6th level, CR 5 at 10th level, and follows the Moon cap at 14th level (starting from CR 1 at 2nd level).
Expend two uses of Wild Shape to transform into an air, earth, fire, or water elemental.
You can cast alter self at will, showcasing complete mastery over mutable form.

moon-combat-wild-shape.summary: Use Wild Shape as a true battle form instead of mostly an exploration tool.
moon-combat-wild-shape.description: Combat Wild Shape is the feature that tells the player this circle fights through transformation. It should make beast form feel like a frontline mode, not a side utility option.

moon-circle-forms.summary: Your available beast forms grow stronger sooner, letting the subclass stay relevant as a shape-based combatant.
moon-circle-forms.description: Circle Forms keeps the Moon druid from falling behind in form quality. It is crucial to making the subclass feel like a real shapeshifting bruiser.

moon-elemental-wild-shape.summary: Your transformations escalate beyond beasts into primal elemental force.
moon-elemental-wild-shape.description: Elemental Wild Shape is a major fantasy spike. It should feel like the subclass breaking through ordinary beast limits into something more mythic and dangerous.

moon-thousand-forms.summary: Alter your appearance with effortless primal fluidity whenever disguise or adaptation matters.
moon-thousand-forms.description: Thousand Forms is a high-level expression of shapeshifting mastery. It expands the circle beyond combat and into social infiltration, mystery, and constant adaptive identity.
